luogu_login.title: Luogu Clipboard Login Credential
luogu_login.desc: Generate a time-limited credential, put it into a public Luogu clipboard, and use that clipboard to sign in directly.
luogu_login.new_clipboard_notice: We recommend creating a new public clipboard for each newly generated credential to improve credential security.
luogu_login.refresh_notice: Only one active credential is kept per account. Generating again refreshes it and immediately invalidates the previous one.
luogu_login.created_copied: New credential generated and copied to clipboard. Previous credential is now invalid

developer.title: Developer Settings
developer.subtitle: Register and manage OAuth applications that integrate with CP OAuth.
developer.secret_warning: Save this client secret now — it will not be shown again.
developer.no_apps: You haven't registered any applications yet.

about.title: Developer Documentation
about.intro: CP OAuth implements the OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code flow with PKCE support. Third-party applications can authenticate users and request granular access to their competitive programming profile data.

flow.title: Authorization Code Flow
flow.description: The standard OAuth2 Authorization Code flow works as follows:
flow.step1: Your application redirects the user to our /oauth/authorize endpoint with your client_id, redirect_uri, and requested scopes.
flow.step2: The user sees a consent screen and decides whether to grant access.
flow.step3: If approved, the user is redirected to your redirect_uri with an authorization code.
flow.step4: Your server exchanges the authorization code for an access token via POST /oauth/token.
flow.step5: Use the access token to call GET /oauth/userinfo and retrieve the user's data.

endpoints.title: API Endpoints
endpoints.authorize_desc: Initiates the authorization flow. Redirects the user to the consent screen.
endpoints.token_desc: Exchanges an authorization code for an access token. Supports both client_secret and PKCE (code_verifier) authentication.
endpoints.userinfo_desc: Returns user data filtered by the scopes granted during authorization.

scopes.title: Permission Scopes
scopes.description: Request only the scopes your application needs. The /oauth/userinfo response is dynamically filtered based on the granted scopes.
scopes.profile: Username, display name, avatar URL, and bio
scopes.cp_linked: All linked competitive programming platform accounts (platform, UID, username)
scopes.link_luogu: Read linked Luogu account info (UID, username)
scopes.link_atcoder: Read linked AtCoder account info (UID, username)
scopes.link_codeforces: Read linked Codeforces account info (UID, username)
scopes.link_github: Read linked GitHub account info (UID, username)
scopes.link_google: Read linked Google account info (UID, username)
scopes.link_clist: Read linked Clist.by account info (UID, username)
scopes.cp_summary: CP Summary — rating, contest count, and ranking per platform via Clist.by (requires linked Clist.by account; platforms must match)
scopes.cp_details: CP Details — rating change history via Clist.by (requires linked Clist.by account; platforms must match)

pkce.title: PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange)
pkce.description: For public clients (e.g. SPAs, mobile apps) that cannot securely store a client_secret, use PKCE instead. Generate a code_verifier, derive a code_challenge using SHA-256, and include it in the authorization request.

users.delete_confirm: Delete this user? This action cannot be undone.
